title: VibeLog Studio MVP
one_line_vibe: A local-first tool for creating, viewing, updating, and exporting Vibe Repos so VibeLog can be dogfooded in real development.
current_idea: VibeLog Studio MVP is the first real dogfood project for VibeLog. It now has a working local-first Slice 1: a user can create a Vibe Repo, view its current state, append a structured update, export Markdown/JSON, and keep data across refresh through browser localStorage.

vibe_intake.problem: VibeLog needs to be tested in a real product workflow, not only as a theoretical standard.
vibe_intake.desired_outcome: A working local MVP that proves a Vibe Repo can be created, updated, viewed, and exported.

idea_expansion.product_hypothesis: If VibeLog Studio can make one Vibe Repo easy to create, update, inspect, and export, then VibeLog has a stronger foundation for future hooks and VibeHub.
idea_expansion.why_it_matters: A standard becomes real only when it survives use inside a real project.
idea_expansion.core_value: Make vibe process memory visible, structured, and portable.
assumptions: local-first is enough for the first proof
Markdown and JSON export are more important than polished UI
two-layer testing is required before a task is complete

idea_evolution.before: VibeLog existed as a skill and standard, but had not yet been used to build a real product from zero.
idea_evolution.after: Use VibeLog Studio MVP as the first dogfood project and record the entire vibe process with VibeLog.
idea_evolution.reason: The user corrected that the real first engineering step should be using the skill to genuinely vibe a project, not building hooks or updater infrastructure first.

idea_evolution.before: The first VibeLog Studio record was pre-implementation and blocked on test-design approval.
idea_evolution.after: The human confirmed the plan, and Slice 1 was implemented as a local-first app under apps/vibelog-studio.
idea_evolution.reason: The user said ??, approving the proposed test gate and engineering direction.

scope_plan.current_goal: Complete Slice 1 of VibeLog Studio MVP: create, view, update, export, persist, and verify a local Vibe Repo flow.
in_scope: Create a Next.js app under apps/vibelog-studio.
Model Vibe Repo domain defaults and update rules.
Persist local Vibe Repos through a repository interface backed by localStorage.
Build a compact app shell for create, list, detail, update, and export preview.
Test each task with single tests and integrated product-flow tests.
Update this dogfood VibeLog with prompts, development logs, bugfixes, verification, and handoff.
out_of_scope: Claude Code or Codex hook automation.
acceptance_criteria: A user can create a local Vibe Repo from title, one-line vibe, and current idea.
The created repo appears in the dashboard and detail view.
A user can append a structured update.
Markdown export includes the key VibeLog sections.
JSON export parses and preserves the same repo identity and status data.
The dogfood VibeLog is updated and JSON parses.
planned_steps: Write failing tests before implementation for domain, exporters, repository, and UI flow.

decisions.decision: Use VibeLog Studio MVP as the first real dogfood project.
decisions.why: It directly tests whether VibeLog can support a real vibe-built product workflow.

decisions.decision: Do not start with hook automation.
decisions.why: First prove the manual/skill-driven VibeLog process inside a real project, then automate painful parts later.

decisions.decision: A task is complete only when both single-function testing and integrated-flow testing pass.
decisions.why: AI-generated work often looks correct in isolation but fails when combined with the product flow.

decisions.decision: Use Next.js, React, TypeScript, Zod, Vitest, Playwright, and localStorage for Slice 1.
decisions.why: This keeps the app close to a future full-stack route while still allowing a small local-first proof.
decisions.source: agent proposal accepted by user confirmation

human_in_the_loop.human_input: The user delegated the project to Codex but required important decisions to be asked, test design to be shown before work, and VibeLog to be called before implementation.
human_in_the_loop.agent_proposal: Codex proposed to start with VibeLog Studio MVP as a real dogfood project.
human_in_the_loop.final_decision: Begin by creating the VibeLog record, then present test design and key decisions before coding.
human_in_the_loop.why_it_mattered: This preserves human ownership of important product and validation decisions while letting Codex handle ordinary execution.
human_in_the_loop.impact: This file was created before product code and became the first dogfood Vibe Repo.

human_in_the_loop.agent_proposal: Start the project workflow by invoking VibeLog first, not by writing product code.
human_in_the_loop.final_decision: Create the VibeLog Studio dogfood record and pause before implementation to show test design.
human_in_the_loop.why_it_mattered: It confirmed the project could start while preserving the agreed pre-work gate.
human_in_the_loop.impact: The first engineering slice became ready for user review.

human_in_the_loop.agent_proposal: Proceed with Slice 1 using the proposed architecture and two-layer completion gate.
human_in_the_loop.final_decision: Codex implemented the first local-first VibeLog Studio slice.
human_in_the_loop.why_it_mattered: It converted the plan into approved execution while keeping the human's validation standard as the release gate.
human_in_the_loop.impact: Product code, tests, E2E flow, and dogfood records were created.

open_questions: Should the first working slice be committed and pushed to GitHub now, or reviewed locally first?
Should the npm audit --omit=dev moderate PostCSS/Next advisory be handled immediately or tracked until a non-breaking Next update is available?
Should Slice 2 prioritize file import/export downloads, schema validation UX, or hook automation?
Should the UI become bilingual before public sharing?

implementation_status.current_state: Slice 1 is implemented locally. The app can create, display, update, export, and persist a Vibe Repo. Tests and browser flow pass. The work is not yet committed or pushed in this session.
completed: Created dogfood VibeLog before product code.
Implemented domain model and update rules.
Implemented repository interface with memory and localStorage implementations.
Added unit, integration, and Playwright E2E tests.
Fixed E2E runner process cleanup on Windows.
Ran desktop and mobile browser visual checks.
Updated VibeLog source and JSON export.
in_progress: Preparing final handoff to the user.
pending: Decide whether to commit and push this slice.
Decide whether to address or track the moderate npm audit advisory.
Add download/import features for real files.
Add schema validation feedback in the app.
Add hook-based automatic VibeLog updates in a later slice.
blocked: Public release remains blocked on human decisions about GitHub push, license, visibility, and audit posture.
next_actions: Review the local app and generated VibeLog.
important_context_for_next_agent: Do not build public community features before the local Vibe Repo loop is stronger.
Do not skip the two-layer completion test.
npm run e2e builds the app, starts next start on port 3100, runs Playwright, and cleans up the process tree.
prompt_visibility remains summary; full prompts are not public by default.
npm audit --omit=dev currently reports 2 moderate issues through Next/PostCSS and suggests a breaking forced fix, so it was not applied.

success_criteria: A user can create a local Vibe Repo from title, one-line vibe, and current idea.
The created Vibe Repo can be viewed in the app.
The user can append at least one structured update.
The app can preview Markdown export.
The app can preview JSON export.
Exported Markdown and JSON describe the same repo.
Local data persists after page refresh.
Each task passes both single-function tests and integrated-flow tests.
core_user_paths: Create Vibe Repo -> view detail -> export Markdown.
Create Vibe Repo -> view detail -> export JSON.
Create Vibe Repo -> append update -> see updated status/history.
Refresh page -> reopen existing Vibe Repo.
manual_test_steps: Create a Vibe Repo with title, one-line vibe, and current idea.
Confirm it appears in the dashboard.
Preview Markdown and inspect key sections.
Preview JSON and verify it parses.
Refresh the browser and confirm the repo remains.
validation_design.automated_test_strategy: Unit tests cover domain defaults, update rules, Markdown export, JSON export, memory/localStorage repository behavior. Integration tests cover create -> update -> export. Playwright E2E covers create -> list -> detail -> append update -> Markdown preview -> JSON preview -> refresh persistence.
edge_cases: Export with no updates beyond the initial idea.
regression_points: Do not lose idea history when updating current idea.
Do not make public visibility the default.
Do not make JSON the source of truth over Markdown.
Do not mark a task complete unless both single and integration tests pass.
Do not leave an E2E server process listening after tests.
risks_safety_privacy: Exported full prompts may contain sensitive information; default prompt visibility should remain summary.
Local persistence can be fragile; export/import should arrive before serious use.
Dependency audit currently has a moderate PostCSS advisory through Next; forced fix is breaking and was not applied.

verification_evidence.evidence_ref: Vitest reported 5 test files passed and 8 tests passed.
verification_evidence.residual_risk: Browser behavior still required E2E verification.

verification_evidence.evidence_ref: Next build completed successfully; Playwright reported 1 Chromium test passed for create, update, export, and reload persistence.
verification_evidence.residual_risk: E2E currently covers one happy path only.

verification_evidence.summary: Browser visual QA passed on desktop and 390px mobile viewport.
verification_evidence.evidence_ref: In-app browser DOM checks found title, create form, fields, repo list, and empty state; screenshots showed no obvious layout break.
verification_evidence.residual_risk: Visual QA did not cover every populated state on mobile.

verification_evidence.summary: npm audit --omit=dev reported 2 moderate vulnerabilities through Next/PostCSS.
verification_evidence.evidence_ref: Audit reported PostCSS <8.5.10 XSS advisory via Next 9.3.4-canary.0 - 16.3.0-canary.5; npm audit fix --force would install a breaking Next version, so no forced fix was applied.
verification_evidence.residual_risk: Track or upgrade when a non-breaking Next/PostCSS path exists.

known_issues: npm audit --omit=dev reports 2 moderate advisories through Next/PostCSS.
No file download/import flow yet; exports are previewed in the UI.
do_not_change: Do not start with public community features.
Do not build hooks before the local Vibe Repo loop is strong enough.
Do not skip the two-layer completion test.
Do not publish private prompts by default.

execution_prompts.prompt_summary: User approved starting the project after requiring VibeLog to be called before implementation and test design to be shown before work.
execution_prompts.result: Created the VibeLog Studio MVP dogfood VibeLog and paused before product implementation to present test design and key decisions.
execution_prompts.reuse_notes: Future implementation should start only after the user approves the test design and key decisions.

execution_prompts.prompt_summary: User confirmed the proposed plan and authorized implementation.
execution_prompts.result: Implemented VibeLog Studio Slice 1 with domain, exporters, persistence, UI, tests, and VibeLog update.
execution_prompts.reuse_notes: Treat short human approvals as execution prompts when they release an agent to perform engineering work.

development_log.summary: Created the initial dogfood VibeLog for VibeLog Studio MVP.
development_log.details: Established the first real Vibe Repo that records the process of building VibeLog Studio MVP.
development_log.verification: JSON parse planned after structured export.
follow_up: Present test design and key decisions to the user before coding.

development_log.details: Defined the app layers, files, TDD workflow, and two-layer completion gate.
development_log.verification: Plan reviewed against the user's requirements before implementation.

development_log.details: Created a Next.js/React/TypeScript app with Vitest and Playwright configuration.

development_log.summary: Implemented Vibe Repo domain model and update rules.
development_log.details: Added defaults for private visibility, summary prompt visibility, core process level, implementation status, validation design, handoff state, idea evolution, and progress updates.
development_log.verification: Domain tests passed and later full Vitest suite passed.
follow_up: Keep schema alignment tight as the public standard evolves.

development_log.details: Exporters convert a VibeRepo into readable Markdown and parseable JSON.
development_log.verification: Exporter tests passed and integration flow confirmed JSON parse.
follow_up: Add real file download/import in Slice 2.

development_log.summary: Implemented repository abstraction and local persistence.
development_log.details: Added VibeRepository, MemoryVibeRepository, and LocalStorageVibeRepository with invalid-payload tolerance.
development_log.verification: Repository tests passed and E2E reload persistence passed.
follow_up: Consider IndexedDB only if localStorage becomes limiting.

development_log.details: Built create form, repo list, detail view, update form, progress list, and Markdown/JSON export preview.
development_log.verification: Playwright E2E passed; browser visual QA passed on desktop and mobile.
follow_up: Add download/import, better populated mobile QA, and schema validation UX.

development_log.details: Covered create -> update -> export at integration level and create -> list -> detail -> update -> export -> reload at browser level.
development_log.verification: npm run test passed with 5 files and 8 tests; npm run e2e passed with 1 browser test.
follow_up: Add edge-case E2E coverage after the core flow stabilizes.

development_log.summary: Fixed E2E runner cleanup on Windows.
development_log.details: Added port cleanup before test start and process-tree cleanup after test end.
development_log.bug_symptom: Playwright could hit stale old app state or fail with port conflicts.
development_log.root_cause: server.kill() killed the shell parent but could leave the Next child process listening on port 3100.
development_log.fix: Added killPort and killProcessTree helpers using PowerShell/taskkill on Windows.
development_log.verification: npm run e2e passed and no Listen socket remained on port 3100.
follow_up: Consider avoiding shell args warning in the runner later.

bugfix_incident_log.symptom: E2E initially timed out or hit stale UI on port 3100.
bugfix_incident_log.root_cause: The test runner killed only the parent shell process; the actual Next server child could remain alive on Windows.
bugfix_incident_log.fix: Updated scripts/run-e2e.mjs to clean the target port before starting and kill the process tree afterward.
prevention_or_follow_up: Keep process-tree cleanup in the runner.
Check for leftover Listen sockets after E2E changes.

bugfix_incident_log.symptom: Playwright strict mode failed because text assertions matched both visible page text and export preview text.
bugfix_incident_log.root_cause: The E2E test used broad full-page text selectors for content duplicated in the Markdown preview.
bugfix_incident_log.fix: Scoped assertions to the detail region and progress region.
bugfix_incident_log.verification: npm run e2e passed after narrowing selectors.
prevention_or_follow_up: Prefer scoped locators for content that also appears in exports.

handoff_state.current_state: VibeLog Studio Slice 1 works locally. The user can run it, create a Vibe Repo, append an update, preview Markdown/JSON, and refresh without losing data. Tests pass. Files are currently local and uncommitted in this session.
completed: Unit, integration, E2E, build, and visual QA completed.
pending: Hook automation design after the manual loop is stronger.
blockers: Release/public sharing decisions remain with the human.
next_actions: Let the user review the slice.
context_for_next_agent: Run npm run test and npm run e2e from apps/vibelog-studio.
Do not skip VibeLog updates after meaningful work.
Record engineering execution prompts, not idea-chat transcripts.
Keep privacy defaults private until the human changes them.

public_private_projection.public_summary: VibeLog Studio MVP is a local-first tool for creating and exporting Vibe Repos.

what_can_be_reused: product concept and public documentation after license decision
what_should_not_be_reused: hidden code until the user changes visibility

vibe_progress.what_happened: Started VibeLog Studio MVP as the first real dogfood project by creating its VibeLog before writing product code.
problems: Must avoid overbuilding and must preserve the pre-implementation test design gate.
next: Present test design and key decisions to the user.

vibe_progress.what_happened: Implemented and verified Slice 1 of VibeLog Studio: create, view, update, export, and persist local Vibe Repos.
problems: E2E runner needed Windows process-tree cleanup.
Broad Playwright text selectors conflicted with export preview content.
Dependency audit reports moderate Next/PostCSS advisory.

public_summary: VibeLog Studio MVP is a small local-first tool for creating, viewing, updating, and exporting Vibe Repos. Its first purpose is to dogfood VibeLog itself by recording the full vibe process from idea to implementation.
